semantic_rules: Sheep can be sheared for wool using shears when they are nearby.
Moving forward in the plains biome can lead to encountering various entities, including sheep, cows, pigs, zombies, and spiders.
The presence of sheep is not guaranteed in the plains biome, requiring continued exploration to find them.
Sheep are not the only entities found in the plains biome, as cows, pigs, zombies, and spiders can also be encountered.
The absence of a sheep in the immediate surroundings does not guarantee their presence in the nearby area, requiring continued exploration.
Sheep are not always immediately visible, requiring extensive exploration to locate them.
The presence of other entities like cows, pigs, zombies, and spiders does not indicate the proximity of sheep.
Sheep can be sheared for wool using shears when they are nearby.
Moving forward in the plains biome can lead to encountering various entities, including sheep, cows, pigs, zombies, and spiders.
The presence of sheep is not guaranteed in the plains biome, requiring continued exploration to find them.
Sheep are not the only entities found in the plains biome, as cows, pigs, zombies, and spiders can also be encountered.
The absence of a sheep in the immediate surroundings does not guarantee their presence in the nearby area, requiring continued exploration.
Sheep are not always immediately visible, requiring extensive exploration to locate them.
The presence of other entities like cows, pigs, zombies, and spiders does not indicate the proximity of sheep.
Sheep can move out of sight when moving forward, requiring continuous exploration to locate them.
The agent's movement forward in the plains biome does not guarantee finding a sheep, even if one was previously in sight.
